commit.message:
feat: register as macOS Open With handler for folders (#48)

Add openwith_darwin.go — CGO Apple Event handler for kAEOpenDocuments.
Installs via init(), drains NSRunLoop for 150ms before Ebitengine starts
so Finder-dispatched events are received on fresh launch.

Uses NSAppleEventDescriptor/NSURL (no deprecated FSRef/FSRefMakePath).

Fix appbundle.go: explicit directory always wins over .app bundle fallback,
enabling both Finder Open With and CLI `zurm /path` to work from .app.

main.go: drainOpenWithEvents() after flag.Parse(), falls back to first
positional CLI arg if no Apple Event path received.

macos/Info.plist: CFBundleDocumentTypes for public.folder (LSHandlerRank:
Alternate — does not steal default handler from Finder).

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Max intelligence auto-play: never repeats dialogue, smart accusation

- Track all dialogue choices per NPC across loops — bot never picks
  the same response twice, always explores unexplored paths first
- Detect dialogue exhaustion: marks NPCs as fully explored when all
  non-end choices have been tried
- Evidence-aware NPC targeting: re-visits NPCs only when new evidence
  unlocks confront nodes (checks all 18 confront node types)
- Smart re-talk logic: limits re-talks to 3/NPC/loop, only when
  new evidence found since last conversation
- Smart accusation: in loop 4+ with enough critical evidence,
  bot accuses Lady Evelyn with correct evidence selection
  (poison_vial, love_letters, secret_passage, brandy_glass)
- Room scoring now factors in confront node availability
- Dialogue tree depth analysis for exhaustion detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(ci): add 4 automated ops workflows — morning briefing, credential health, stale scan, doc drift

Permanent GitHub Actions replacing session-scoped Claude cron:
- Morning Ops Briefing: daily 8:17 AM CDT, checks 5 services + recent commits
- Credential Health: weekly Monday 10:42 AM, verifies 6 service endpoints
- Stale Reference Scan: weekly Wednesday 11:22 AM, greps for deprecated refs
- Doc Drift Scan: daily 9:03 AM, checks pricing/port/service contradictions

All create GitHub issues on failures. All support manual workflow_dispatch.

Step 3.4: Entity API endpoints — list, detail, related, graph explore

Add GET /entities (paginated, type-filterable), GET /entities/{id}
(detail with documents and related entities), GET /entities/{id}/related
(graph traversal), and GET /graph/explore (graph exploration from starting
entity). Replace placeholder /documents/{id}/entities with real data.

New: EntityService, entity_schemas.py, entities.py (entities_router +
graph_router), count() on EntityRepository. 8 unit tests, inspection script.

cause of all provisioning failures.

commit.message:
Fix black screen on Spring months; add fog-of-war and landmarks to backlog

- Root cause: destroyed Text object from previous month still held reference
  in this.objText; !destroyed_object is falsy so guard passed, then setText
  tried drawImage on a null canvas → crash. Fix: declare as T|null=null and
  reset in init() so the guard works correctly on scene transitions.
- Backlog: Fog of War (reveal maze as you explore) and decorative landmark
  sprites (pond, windmill, hollow tree, snowman, etc.) per season.

Fix last rep stuck in pending due to SSE chunk splitting

was silently dropped. Hoist currentEvent above the loop.

commit.message:
F.O.R.G.E. plugin v1.0.0 — security-first development skills for Claude Code

Six skills powered by the F.O.R.G.E. framework and SEED philosophy:
- forge:audit — structured security audits with OWASP coverage
- forge:threat — STRIDE threat modeling for architectures
- forge:harden — real-time secure code review with inline fixes
- forge:recon — attack surface mapping and enumeration
- forge:seed — safe experimentation guidance with blast radius containment
- forge:protocol — ASSESS-PLAN-BUILD-VERIFY development workflow

Eval results: 24/24 assertions passed (100%) vs 23/24 baseline (95.8%).
Key differentiator: forge:seed transforms Claude from risk-focused gatekeeper
to encouraging guide for safe experimentation.

Built by zero-lab.ai. Security from operators, for everyone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix data race on running_ in WorkStealingThreadPool::join()

Plain read of atomically-modified variable is UB. Use atomic load
with Acquire ordering to match the Relaxed atomic decrement in
Worker::run().

commit.message:
fix: auto-seed demo accounts on Lambda cold start

File-based storage on Amplify SSR is ephemeral — /tmp/ data disappears
when Lambda instances recycle. This seeds demo accounts automatically
whenever users.json doesn't exist, so logins always work.

commit.message:
Fix privacy policy for AdSense compliance + add 3 new guides

Privacy policy fixes:
- Added DoubleClick mention (required by AdSense)
- Added aboutads.info opt-out link (required)
- Added Network Advertising Initiative opt-out link
- Added "How Google uses data" partner sites link (required)
- Added International data transfers section
- Added Security measures section
- Updated date to March 11, 2026

New guides (~800-1000 words each):
- /guides/color-theory-fundamentals — color wheel, HSL/RGB/CMYK, harmonies
- /guides/accessible-color-design — color blindness, WCAG 1.4.1/1.4.3, inclusive design
- /guides/color-in-branding — brand psychology, color systems, industry conventions

Sitemap and footer updated with all 3 new guide routes.
